title: Experience Overhaul
subtitle: Enhancing product discoverability and reducing cognitive load for a seamless user journey.
problem_statement: The current visual hierarchy, particularly the small 'Sign Up | Log In' links and the dense promotional banner text ('Enjoy 10% Off On The Nike App. User: APP10 Download Now T&Cs'), creates unnecessary cognitive load for new users and potentially obscures key calls to action. The dominant hero imagery ('MORE CHOICE. MORE RUNNING.'), while impactful, pushes immediate product exploration below the fold, leading to potential legacy failures in direct product access and efficient browsing.

pillars.title: Cognitive Ease
pillars.insight: Users seek effortless information processing; small, clustered text like 'Help | Sign Up | Log In' increases mental effort and makes essential actions harder to find.

pillars.title: Visual Clarity
pillars.insight: An effective visual hierarchy guides attention; the large hero image, while engaging, can overshadow other critical content and push product categories out of immediate view.

pillars.title: Efficiency Drive
pillars.insight: Users value quick task completion; navigating past large banners and finding specific products or categories can feel time-consuming due to excessive scrolling.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we streamline the top-right utility navigation to reduce cognitive load for new and returning users, making 'Sign Up' and 'Log In' more prominent?
How might we balance impactful hero imagery with immediate product discoverability to cater to diverse user intents, offering quicker paths to specific categories?
How might we integrate promotional content like 'Enjoy 10% Off On The Nike App' more seamlessly without distracting from core product exploration or adding visual clutter?

points.action: Replace the small 'Sign Up | Log In' text with distinct, larger buttons or a clear 'My Account' dropdown in the header.
points.benefit: Reduces cognitive load and improves discoverability for essential user actions, especially for new users, by making them visually prominent.

points.action: Integrate the promotional banner ('Enjoy 10% Off...') into a more visually appealing, less text-heavy notification bar or a subtle hero overlay with a clear, concise message.
points.benefit: Reduces visual clutter and allows the main hero content to shine, while still conveying important offers without distraction or dense text.

points.action: Adapt the 'Featured' section to include a 'Quick Shop' or 'Browse by Category' carousel directly below the hero, offering immediate product access.
points.benefit: Caters to users with high intent, reducing the need to scroll extensively to find specific product types and improving efficiency.

points.action: Modify the hero section to include a secondary, smaller call-to-action for 'Explore All Running Products' alongside the main 'Shop' button, or a subtle category filter.
points.benefit: Provides more pathways for exploration directly from the hero, catering to both broad browsing and specific intent, enhancing user control.

pivot.before: The current design prioritizes a single, dominant visual narrative (e.g., 'MORE CHOICE. MORE RUNNING.') and large featured blocks ('Pegasus 42', 'Manika Batra Athlete Picks'), which, while aesthetically pleasing, creates a legacy failure in immediate product discoverability and efficient navigation for users with specific purchase intent. The small utility links ('Sign Up | Log In') also add cognitive friction.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shift from a purely aspirational, scroll-heavy visual experience to a balanced approach that integrates immediate utility and diverse product entry points. This means making essential actions more prominent and offering quicker paths to product categories or specific items, reducing the cognitive load associated with finding information or products.
pivot.after: A redesigned homepage that maintains strong brand imagery but strategically places more prominent utility links (e.g., distinct 'Sign Up', 'Log In' buttons), a more integrated and less intrusive promotional banner, and immediate, visually distinct pathways to key product categories or quick-shop carousels directly below the hero, enhancing efficiency and reducing friction.

annotations.title: Enhanced Utility Navigation
annotations.rationale: The current 'Help | Sign Up | Log In' is too small. Redesigning with distinct 'Sign Up' and 'Log In' buttons or a 'My Account' dropdown improves visibility and reduces cognitive load for new and returning users, making these critical actions easily accessible.

annotations.title: Integrated Promotional Banner
annotations.rationale: The current banner's small text and 'T&Cs' link are distracting. A more subtle, visually integrated banner (e.g., a notification bar or a less text-heavy overlay) reduces visual clutter while still conveying offers effectively without demanding excessive attention.

annotations.title: Immediate Product Entry Points
annotations.rationale: The large hero and featured blocks push product discovery down. Introducing a 'Quick Shop' carousel or prominent category links directly below the hero allows users with specific intent to bypass scrolling and find products faster, improving efficiency.
